rationale: The citing paper discusses Briesch, Chintagunta, and Fox (2009) in a dedicated literature-review paragraph and in Table 1, summarizing its structural evidence that assortment dimensions materially affect store choice and can matter more than price. This is used to position the new "canary category" contribution within the assortment-store-choice stream and to motivate why assortment gaps can be linked to retention/churn outcomes. The citation is more than a passing list mention, but it is used for conceptual and empirical framing rather than direct method reuse.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Briesch et al. (2009) as prior foundational evidence that assortment composition influences store choice behavior. It builds on that framing to study a related but distinct outcome, showing how category purchases can signal future nonreturn and churn when preferred brands are missing.
